import argparse
import subprocess
import sys
import pathlib
from typing import List, Optional


def run(cmd: List[str], cwd: Optional[pathlib.Path] = None) -> int:
    process = subprocess.Popen(
        cmd,
        cwd=str(cwd) if cwd else None,
        stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
        stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
        text=True,
    )
    assert process.stdout is not None
    for line in process.stdout:
        sys.stdout.write(line)
    process.wait()
    return process.returncode


def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Fast lane regression check runner."
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--tests",
        "-t",
        nargs="*",
        default=[],
        help="Specific tests or directories to run (pytest style).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--marker",
        "-m",
        default="fast",
        help="Pytest marker to use for fast-lane regression (default: fast).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--no-marker",
        action="store_true",
        help="Do not filter by marker; run given tests directly.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--extra-args",
        nargs=argparse.REMAINDER,
        help="Extra arguments passed through to pytest.",
    )
    return parser.parse_args()


def build_pytest_command(args: argparse.Namespace) -> List[str]:
    cmd = [sys.executable, "-m", "pytest"]
    if args.tests:
        cmd.extend(args.tests)
    if not args.no_marker and args.marker:
        cmd.extend(["-m", args.marker])
    if args.extra_args:
        cmd.extend(args.extra_args)
    return cmd


def main() -> int:
    args = parse_args()
    pytest_cmd = build_pytest_command(args)
    print("Running fast lane regression check:")
    print(" ", " ".join(pytest_cmd))
    code = run(pytest_cmd)
    if code != 0:
        print(f"Fast lane regression check failed with exit code {code}")
    else:
        print("Fast lane regression check passed")
    return code


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
